findings.summary: ImageEditState is a 60+ field mega-context spanning canvas, form, status, and reposition concerns, assembled manually in two places
evidence: ImageEditState is the intersection of ImageEditCanvasState, ImageEditFormState, and ImageEditStatusState
Composed type assembled manually in useImageEditOrchestrator (lines 415-547) and useInlineEditState (lines 334-411), each mapping 60+ fields
useMemo dependency array in useImageEditOrchestrator has 46 entries (lines 525-547)
Both files contain nearly identical code assembling the same ImageEditState shape from the same sub-hooks
findings.suggestion: Extract a shared buildImageEditState() factory that takes the four sub-hook results and returns ImageEditState, eliminating the duplicated 60+ field assembly.

findings.summary: buildLightboxVideoModeReturn is a 60-line function that copies every field without transformation
evidence: Lines 166-229: destructures all fields from input, returns them in identical shape
BuildLightboxVideoModeReturnInput interface mirrors UseLightboxVideoModeReturn almost exactly
Function performs zero transformation -- every output field is a direct copy
Used only once (line 344), making it pure indirection with no reuse benefit
findings.suggestion: Delete buildLightboxVideoModeReturn and its input interface. Return the result object directly from useLightboxVideoMode.

findings.summary: setPayloadField/setPayloadFieldIfDefined/setPayloadFieldIf are trivial wrappers over property assignment
evidence: setPayloadField (line 12-18): payload[key] = value -- one-line function wrapping single assignment
setPayloadFieldIfDefined: if (value !== undefined) { payload[key] = value } -- trivial conditional
setPayloadField (unconditional variant) exported but never imported by any src/ consumer
Combined helpers used in only 4 src files
findings.suggestion: Inline the trivial helpers. Keep only setTaskLineageFields since it encodes a meaningful lineage contract.

findings.summary: useSharedLightboxState exports 20 symbols including renamed aliases and sub-facade hooks, 812 lines
evidence: 812 lines with 20 exports including interfaces, hooks, and aliases
Alias exports that add no value: useSharedNavigationState = useLightboxNavigationModel, useSharedShotState = useLightboxShotActions, useSharedLightboxPresentationState = useLightboxPanelModel
Sub-facade hooks exported as 'usable standalone' but only consumed internally
findings.suggestion: Remove the three alias exports. If sub-facade hooks are only consumed internally, make them non-exported. Move prop interfaces to a separate types file.

findings.summary: useVideoEditModeHandlers queries all <video> DOM elements to capture duration instead of using a ref
evidence: Line 66-75: captureVideoDuration uses setTimeout + document.querySelectorAll('video')
Reads duration from ALL video elements on the page, not the specific lightbox video
The lightbox already has a trimVideoRef that could be threaded through
100ms setTimeout is a timing hack; ref-based approach would be event-driven
findings.suggestion: Pass trimVideoRef through to useVideoEditModeHandlers and read duration from the ref directly.

findings.summary: cancelPendingTasks queries the same tasks table twice with identical filters, different columns
evidence: Lines 113-116: First query selects 'id, task_type' WHERE project_id=X AND status IN ['Queued']
Lines 135-138: Second query selects 'id, params' with identical filter
A single query selecting 'id, task_type, params' would eliminate the round-trip
findings.suggestion: Combine into a single query selecting 'id, task_type, params' and split results in application code.

findings.summary: SegmentSlotModeData is a 40+ field interface mixing navigation, generation config, structure video, and callbacks
evidence: Lines 112-215 of types.ts: ~40 fields spanning navigation, pair data, prompts, structure video config, frame management, and UI callbacks
Crosses concerns: rendering context, generation params, persistence, and interaction
Every consumer must thread the entire mega-interface even when only needing a subset
findings.suggestion: Split into focused sub-interfaces: SegmentNavigation, SegmentPairData, SegmentPrompts, SegmentStructureVideo, SegmentCallbacks. Compose at call site.

findings.summary: SharedGenerationView (467 lines) mixes auth logic, RPC calls, data transforms, settings display, and floating CTA
evidence: Lines 64-104: Auth checking with onAuthStateChange subscription
Lines 106-146: RPC call with error handling and navigation
Lines 150-175: Data transformation and settings extraction
Lines 177-466: Full render tree with inline sliders, controls, form, and modal
findings.suggestion: Extract auth + copy-to-account logic into a useShareActions hook. Extract settings display into ShareSettingsView. Keep SharedGenerationView as thin composition.

findings.summary: useEditSettingsPersistence (387 lines) mixes per-generation, last-used, video, and panel mode concerns, returning 35+ fields
evidence: Manages per-generation settings, last-used settings, video enhance settings, panel mode, and LoRA resolution
Return interface has 35+ fields: 17 values + 17 setters + state flags + legacy compatibility
Video enhance settings unrelated to image editing
Panel mode is a layout/navigation concern mixed with settings persistence
findings.suggestion: Split into useImageEditPersistence, useLastUsedEditSettings (already exists as sub-hook), and move video/panel mode persistence to video lightbox environment.

findings.summary: OperationResult type system (4 types, 1 error class, 3 factory functions) used in only 5 consumer files
evidence: Defines OperationResult, OperationSuccess, OperationFailure, OperationResultError, factory functions, and FailurePolicy
FailurePolicy has 4 variants but OperationSuccess.policy is always 'best_effort' (default)
Factory functions used in only 2 files (both in supabase/functions/)
Most error handling uses normalizeAndPresentError directly
findings.suggestion: Either commit to using OperationResult broadly or simplify to a lighter Result<T> union without unused policy/recoverable metadata.

findings.summary: Bidirectional dependency between src/shared/ (1621 files) and src/features/ (34 files)
evidence: 27 imports from @/features/ found in src/shared/ across billing, layout, lora, gallery, shots, account, ai, lightbox, resources, settings
All 17 src/features/ files import from @/shared/
features/ is 34 files vs shared/ at 1621 files -- incomplete extraction
Both directions carry runtime dependencies, not just type imports
findings.suggestion: Either absorb features/ back into shared/ since extraction is incomplete, or commit by moving shared code that depends on features (CreditsManagement, LoraManager, GenerationsPane) into features/ and make shared/ a true leaf dependency.

findings.summary: shared/ depends on tools/ indirectly via tooling/toolDefaultsRegistry, creating hidden backwards dependency
evidence: toolDefaultsRegistry.ts imports from @/tools/travel-between-images/settings, @/tools/image-generation/settings, etc.
shared/lib/toolSettingsService.ts and shared/hooks/usePersistentToolState.ts import from @/tooling/toolDefaultsRegistry
Creates dependency chain: shared -> tooling -> tools, violating shared as leaf principle
findings.suggestion: Move all tool settings defaults into shared/, have toolDefaultsRegistry live in shared/. Tools settings files re-export from shared for local convenience.

findings.summary: Substantial tool-specific code in shared/: JoinClipsSettingsForm (15+ files), travel task builders (9 files), tool-specific TaskDetails
evidence: JoinClipsSettingsForm/ is a full component directory (15+ files) in shared/ with tool-specific UI logic
shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/ is 9 files with travel-specific payload building
TaskDetails has tool-specific components: VideoTravelDetails, JoinClipsDetails, CharacterAnimateDetails
Segment hooks hardcode TOOL_IDS.TRAVEL_BETWEEN_IMAGES four times
findings.suggestion: UI components like JoinClipsSettingsForm and tool-specific TaskDetails views should move to their respective tools/, with shared/ using a plugin/registry pattern.

findings.summary: features/image-edit/ is a single 79-line barrel file re-exporting everything from shared/components/MediaLightbox/
evidence: Only file in the directory, re-exports from 10+ paths inside shared/components/MediaLightbox/
Only 2 consumers: tools/edit-images/hooks/useInlineEditState.ts and tools/edit-images/components/InlineEditView.tsx
tools/edit-video imports the same internals directly from shared/components/MediaLightbox/ without this barrel
findings.suggestion: Either make this a real feature module by moving image-edit hooks out of MediaLightbox, or remove the barrel and have tools/edit-images import directly like tools/edit-video does.

findings.summary: MediaLightbox is 232 files in shared/ -- larger than many tools, functioning as a feature module
evidence: 232 files vs 498 total in all of src/tools/
Contains hooks/, components/, contexts/, utils/ -- full feature module structure
Has tool-specific logic (useJoinClips.ts, useVideoEditing.ts with TOOL_IDS.EDIT_VIDEO references)
findings.suggestion: Extract into its own feature module (features/lightbox/) with a clear public API. Move tool-specific hooks to their respective tools.

findings.summary: shared/hooks/ imports types and utilities from shared/components/ internals, inverting expected dependency direction
evidence: useSegmentSettings.ts, useSegmentMutations.ts, usePairMetadata.ts import from shared/components/SegmentSettingsForm/
useSegmentMutations.ts imports buildMetadataUpdate (runtime function) from SegmentSettingsForm/segmentSettingsMigration
useLoadVariantImages.ts imports from components/VariantSelector/variantSourceImages
findings.suggestion: Extract types (SegmentSettings, PairMetadata, etc.) and utility functions (buildMetadataUpdate) into shared/types/ or shared/lib/.

findings.summary: useLoraManager hook imports and renders a React component (LoraHeaderActions), blurring hook/component boundary
evidence: useLoraManager.tsx imports LoraHeaderActions from @/shared/components/LoraHeaderActions
Hook's renderHeaderActions callback returns JSX with <LoraHeaderActions ... />
This is a .tsx file in hooks directory -- hooks should return data/callbacks, not rendered UI
findings.suggestion: Have the hook return data/state needed for rendering and let the consuming component render LoraHeaderActions itself.

findings.summary: shared/lib/taskConfig.ts imports from shared/hooks/tasks/useTaskType, violating lib < hooks layering
evidence: taskConfig.ts imports getTaskTypeConfigCache, isTaskTypeConfigCacheInitialized from hooks/tasks/useTaskType
lib/ should be lower layer than hooks/ -- hooks can depend on lib but not vice versa
Imported functions are module-level cache accessors co-located with a React hook
findings.suggestion: Extract cache accessors and TaskTypeInfo into shared/lib/taskTypeCache.ts. The hook populates the cache, taskConfig reads from it.

findings.summary: Shared segment hooks hardcode TOOL_IDS.TRAVEL_BETWEEN_IMAGES, coupling shared code to a specific tool
evidence: useSegmentMutations.ts references TOOL_IDS.TRAVEL_BETWEEN_IMAGES four times for toolId and cache invalidation
useShotVideoSettings.ts reads settings specifically from allSettings[TOOL_IDS.TRAVEL_BETWEEN_IMAGES]
Shared hooks should be tool-agnostic but assume they serve only the travel tool
findings.suggestion: Accept toolId as a parameter rather than hardcoding it, allowing these hooks to be truly reusable.

findings.summary: Three realtime singletons instantiate at module level with cross-dependencies between them
evidence: RealtimeConnection.ts line 517: export const realtimeConnection = new RealtimeConnection() runs at import time
DataFreshnessManager.ts line 362: export const dataFreshnessManager = new DataFreshnessManager() runs at import time
RealtimeEventProcessor.ts line 461: export const realtimeEventProcessor = new RealtimeEventProcessor() runs at import time
RealtimeConnection constructor attaches window event listener via listenAppEvent at import time
RealtimeConnection imports dataFreshnessManager, creating cross-singleton import-time dependency
findings.suggestion: Convert to lazy-initialized singletons via getter functions (like getReconnectScheduler pattern already used elsewhere) to avoid constructor side effects at import time.

findings.summary: PreloadingService singleton subscribes to DataFreshnessManager during module-level instantiation
evidence: service.ts line 290: export const preloadingService = PreloadingService.getInstance() runs at import time
Constructor calls subscribeToConnectionStatus() which calls dataFreshnessManager.getDiagnostics() and subscribe()
Creates hidden initialization-order dependency between PreloadingService and DataFreshnessManager
findings.suggestion: Use lazy initialization (getter function) for preloadingService, or defer subscription to DataFreshnessManager until first use.

findings.summary: PreloadingService exposes itself on window in production, not gated behind DEV check
evidence: service.ts lines 293-296: window.__PRELOADING_SERVICE__ = preloadingService runs unconditionally
Unlike debugConfig, debugPolling, simpleCacheValidator, and mobileProjectDebug which all gate behind import.meta.env.DEV
Leaks internal state to production window globals
findings.suggestion: Gate the window assignment behind import.meta.env.DEV to match the pattern used by every other debug global.

findings.summary: debugConfig attaches itself to window unconditionally (not gated behind DEV)
evidence: debugConfig.ts lines 154-156: window.debugConfig = debugConfig runs at module level, no DEV check
Other debug tools all check import.meta.env.DEV before attaching to window
Inconsistent and leaks debug controls to production
findings.suggestion: Add import.meta.env.DEV && to the guard condition, matching debugPolling and simpleCacheValidator.

findings.summary: settingsWriteQueue registers window/document event listeners at import time before write function is injected
evidence: Lines 222-232: module-level block registers beforeunload and visibilitychange listeners
Listeners call flushAll() which depends on writeFunction being set via setSettingsWriteFunction()
If module imported before bootstrap, listeners exist but writeFunction is null
findings.suggestion: Move event listener registration into setSettingsWriteFunction() call, or defer to first enqueue call.

findings.summary: TaskTypeConfigInitializer renders outside AuthGate, firing Supabase query before authentication
evidence: AppProviders.tsx line 55: TaskTypeConfigInitializer rendered as sibling of AppProviderTree, not inside it
AppProviderTree includes AuthProvider and AuthGate as first two providers
TaskTypeConfigInitializer fires supabase().from('task_types') query before auth verification
Query will likely fail or return empty data for unauthenticated users, wasting a network request
findings.suggestion: Move TaskTypeConfigInitializer inside AppProviderTree after AuthGate, or make query skip when no auth session exists.
